title: Unit 1: Limits and Continuity
description: Introduce the foundational concept of limits, how they describe function behavior near a point, and the notion of continuity. Students will learn algebraic techniques for evaluating limits and understand why continuity matters for calculus.
auto_prompt: Detailed content instructions: create Lesson 1.1: "Understanding Limits Intuitively" and Lesson 1.2: "Algebraic Limit Techniques" and Lesson 1.3: "Continuity and the Intermediate Value Theorem". Each lesson subpage should contain quiz blocks testing limit computation, one-step and multi-step algebraic manipulations, and continuity identification; embed a Desmos visualization illustrating approaching points and jump/discontinuous behavior; provide a chatbot tutor block with the persona of a patient university math TA; include real web citations from reputable sources (e.g., Khan Academy, Wolfram MathWorld); and add text analysis blocks that highlight key definitions and common misconceptions. Be explicit about the quiz question types (multiple choice, fill‑in‑the‑blank) and the visual interaction steps.

title: Unit 2: Derivatives Fundamentals
description: Develop the derivative as a rate of change and slope of a tangent line. Cover formal definition, basic differentiation rules, and applications to motion and optimization.
auto_prompt: Detailed content instructions: list Lesson 2.1: "The Derivative as a Limit", Lesson 2.2: "Power, Constant, and Sum Rules", Lesson 2.3: "Product and Quotient Rules", and Lesson 2.4: "Applications: Tangent Lines and Velocity". For each lesson include quizzes that assess limit‑based derivative calculations, rule application, and real‑world problem solving; embed Desmos graphs showing secant-to-tangent transitions and dynamic slope sliders; assign a chatbot tutor persona of an enthusiastic physics‑oriented instructor; cite sources such as MIT OpenCourseWare and Paul's Online Math Notes; and provide text analysis blocks that extract formulae and highlight step‑by‑step reasoning.

title: Unit 3: Techniques of Differentiation
description: Expand differentiation tools to include chain rule, implicit differentiation, and derivatives of trigonometric, exponential, and logarithmic functions. Emphasize problem‑solving strategies for complex expressions.
auto_prompt: Detailed content instructions: create Lesson 3.1: "Chain Rule and Composite Functions", Lesson 3.2: "Implicit Differentiation and Related Rates", Lesson 3.3: "Derivatives of Trig, Exponential, and Log Functions", and Lesson 3.4: "Higher‑Order Derivatives and Motion Problems". Each lesson must feature quizzes that test multi‑layer chain applications, implicit differentiation steps, and derivative tables; integrate Desmos visualizations that animate inner‑outer function changes and curve families; provide a chatbot tutor with the persona of a seasoned engineering professor; reference authoritative web pages (e.g., MathIsFun, Coursera lecture notes); and include text analysis blocks that compare derivative formulas and flag common algebraic errors.

title: Unit 4: Introduction to Integration
description: Present the definite integral as accumulated area and the Fundamental Theorem of Calculus linking differentiation and integration. Cover basic antiderivative techniques and simple applications.
auto_prompt: Detailed content instructions: outline Lesson 4.1: "Area Under a Curve and Riemann Sums", Lesson 4.2: "The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus", Lesson 4.3: "Basic Antiderivative Rules", and Lesson 4.4: "Applications: Net Change and Simple Volumes". For every lesson embed quizzes on Riemann sum calculations, theorem interpretation, and antiderivative identification; provide Desmos widgets that let students adjust partition sizes and see convergence; assign a chatbot tutor persona of a friendly calculus historian; cite web resources such as the OpenStax Calculus textbook and Khan Academy videos; and add text analysis blocks that summarize theorem statements and illustrate stepwise integration processes.
